name: HMG
subtitle: Human Menopausal Gonadotropin | FSH/LH Fertility Hormone
overview: Human Menopausal Gonadotropin (HMG) is a hormonally active medication containing follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and luteinizing hormone (LH) in a 1:1 ratio, extracted from the urine of postmenopausal women. FDA-approved for fertility treatment, HMG stimulates ovarian follicle development in women and spermatogenesis in men. While largely replaced by recombinant gonadotropins in some settings, HMG remains an effective and cost-efficient option for ovulation induction and assisted reproduction.
mechanism: HMG acts on gonadal tissue through two mechanisms: FSH stimulates growth and maturation of ovarian follicles containing eggs in women, and promotes spermatogenesis in men. LH stimulates ovulation and corpus luteum formation in women, and Leydig cells in men to produce testosterone. Highly purified HMG (HP-hMG) has enhanced FSH receptor activity with reduced inactive proteins. The LH component modifies follicular development and decreases intermediate-sized follicles, potentially resulting in safer, more controlled stimulation.
keyBenefits: Contains both FSH and LH naturally
LH activity may improve fertilization rates

items.name: Ovulation Induction
items.description: Stimulates follicular development in anovulatory women.

items.name: IVF Stimulation
items.description: Used in IVF protocols to stimulate multiple follicle development.

items.name: PCOS Ovulation
items.description: Effective for inducing ovulation in polycystic ovary syndrome patients.

items.name: Spermatogenesis
items.description: FSH promotes sperm production; LH stimulates testosterone for sperm development.

items.name: Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism
items.description: Treats male infertility due to insufficient gonadotropin production.

items.name: LH Replacement
items.description: LH component stimulates testicular testosterone production.

deliveryMethods.overview: HMG is administered via intramuscular or subcutaneous injection. Treatment typically starts on day 2-3 of the menstrual cycle and continues for 7-12 days with monitoring. Dosing is individualized based on follicular response monitored by ultrasound and estradiol levels.

steps: Add provided diluent to powder vial

keyBenefits: Can combine with other fertility medications

interactions.notes: hCG is typically used to trigger ovulation after HMG-induced follicular development.
Often used in combination protocols for IVF to prevent premature LH surge.
May be used in sequential protocols for ovulation induction.
Different mechanisms; may be used in different fertility protocols.

timeline.effects: Follicles reach mature size; trigger ovulation
Ovulation occurs ~36 hours after hCG trigger

contraindications: Abnormal uterine bleeding of unknown cause
monitoring: Regular ultrasound monitoring of follicular development

references.title: Human Menopausal Gonadotropin
references.keyFindings: HMG contains FSH and LH in 1:1 ratio extracted from postmenopausal urine.

references.title: HMG vs Recombinant FSH in PCOS Patients Undergoing IVF
references.keyFindings: No significant differences in pregnancy rates between FSH alone, rFSH+HMG, and rFSH+rLH groups.

references.title: Effect of HMG and HP-FSH on IVF Outcomes
references.keyFindings: Beneficial effects of HMG on fertilization rates and pre-embryo development compared to HP-FSH.

references.title: Generic hMG vs Costly FSH for Ovulation Induction
references.keyFindings: Generic hMG products do not adversely affect pregnancy rates and are appropriate cost-effective alternatives.

quickStats.typicalDose: 75-150 IU for ovulation induction; 150-300 IU for IVF
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: Room temperature. Reconstituted: 2-8°C, use immediately after reconstitution
